title: Poitea gracilis
intro: Poitea gracilis, also known as the New Zealand kawakawa or kawa kawa, is a shrub native to New Zealand, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and pain relief. The bioactive constituents of Poitea gracilis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, such as kawain and desmethylstylopine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Poitea gracilis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in traditional New Zealand medicine to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Poitea gracilis is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional Māori medicine. The plant is also known as the 'tītī flower' or 'New Zealand tītī'. Poitea gracilis is classified as a perennial herb, growing in a variety of habitats. Its classification at the genus level places it within the tribe Loteae.

side_effects: Poitea gracilis, also known as the 'kawakawa', is a plant native to New Zealand with a rich history of traditional use.
